concepts.title: Basic functionality of inverse sine function
concepts.conceptBody:
Inverse Sine Function 

The inverse sine function is defined by $y=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$ if and only if $x=\text{sin}y$ where

$-1\le \text{x}\le 1$ and $-\frac{\pi }{2}\le \text{y}\le \frac{\pi }{2}$

In definition, the equation $\text{y}=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}$ can be read by as $\text{y}$ is the angle whose sine is

$\text{x}$. Therefore, $\text{y}=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{1}{2}$ means by $\text{y}$ is the angle, between $-\frac{\pi }{2}$ and $\frac{\pi }{2}$, inclusive, whose $\text{sin}$ is $\frac{1}{2}$, thus $\text{y}=\frac{\pi }{6}$.

(The angle could also be expressed as 30 .)

Principal value of $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}$ itself its value as inverse functions are consider as function only for a particular interval. So their is no need to define separate principal value.

concepts.title: Basic functionality of inverse cosine function
concepts.conceptBody:
Inverse Cosine Function 

The other trigonometric functions can also be used to introduce inverse function. In each

case, a restriction needs to be place on the original domain to create a one-to-one function

that contains the entire range of the original function. Then a corresponding inverse

function can be defined. By restricting the domain of the cosine function to real numbers

between 0 and $\pi $, inclusive, a one-to-one function with a range between -1 and 1 ,

inclusive, is obtained. Then the following definition creates the inverse cosine function.

The inverse cosine function or are cosine function is defined by

$\text{y}=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\text{x}=\text{arccosx }\!\!~\!\!\text{ if }\!\!~\!\!\text{ and }\!\!~\!\!\text{ only }\!\!~\!\!\text{ if }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}=\text{cosy}$

Where $-1\le x\le 1\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ and }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }0\le y\le \pi $

concepts.title: Basic functionality of inverse tangent function
concepts.conceptBody:
Inverse Tangent Function

By restricting the domain of the tangent function to real numbers between $-\left( \pi /2 \right)$

and $\pi /2,-\left( \pi /2 \right)$ and $\pi /2$ are not included since the tangent is undefined at those

values) a one-to-one function is obtained. Therefore, the inverse tangent function.

The inverse tangent function or arctangent function is defined by

$y=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\text{arc tan }x \text{ if } \text{ and } \text{ only } \text{ if } x=\text{tan }y$

Where $-\infty <\text{x}<\infty  \text{ and } -\left( \pi /2 \right)<\text{y}<\pi /2$

concepts.title: Properties on arcsecx
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties on arc secx

(v) $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( \text{sec}x \right)=x,\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $0\le x<-\frac{\pi }{2}$ or $\frac{\pi }{2}<x\le \pi $

concepts.title: Properties of inverse trigonometric functions part 1
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of inverse trigonometric functions

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sin}\left( \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x} \right)=\text{x},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-1\le \text{x}\le 1$

Let $\text{sin}\theta =\text{x}$ …….(1)

$\theta =\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$ …….(2)

Substituting the value of $\theta $ in (1) from (2), $\text{sin}\left( \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x \right)=x$.

Here $\text{x}$ is a number and will have no unit.

(ii) $\text{cos}\left( \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\text{x} \right)=\text{x},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Provided that $-1\le \text{x}\le 1$

(iii) $\text{tan}\left( \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x} \right)=\text{x}$, 

Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}<\infty $

(iv) $\text{cot}\left( \text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}\text{x} \right)=\text{x}$, 

Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}<\infty $

(v) $\text{sec}\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}} \right)\text{x}=\text{x}$, 

Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}\le 1$ or $1\le \text{x}<\infty $

(iv) $\text{cosec}\left( \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\text{x} \right)=\text{x}$, 

Provided that $-\infty <\text{x}\le -1$ or $1\le \text{x}<\infty $
